content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF
BAGATELOS & FADEM
THE INTERNATIONAL BUILDING
601 CALIFORNIA STREET
SUITE 1801
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94108

BARRY FADEM
PETER A. BAGATELOS

TELEPHONE
(415) 982-7100
FAX
(415) 982-1085

October 29, 1990

Mr. Mark Morodomi
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA    95814


Dear Mr. Morodomi:

     This will confirm our telephone conversation on October 15,
1990.  On that date, you provided me with legal advice in response
to facts which I had previously presented to you in several prior
telephone conversations.  I shall briefly recite the basic facts
and then confirm the advice you gave me.

     If you recall, I initially called with a general inquiry
regarding this general subject matter area and merely requested
general guidance from you.  During the course of several telephone
conversations, you required me to disclose the name of my client
and the specific background facts in order for you and your
colleagues to be able to provide me with a response.  While I found
this quite unusual, having never been required by the FPPC when I
have sought advice over the last fourteen years to provide details
regarding my client in order first to obtain advice, I nevertheless
received permission from my client to provide the information and
did so.  At my request, you did check with others in your office
and later advised me that, despite your earlier requirement that I
provide particular facts to you, it was confirmed that I and others
like me could still inquire and receive general advice and guidance
on an informal basis without disclosing particular facts.

     I advised you that I am representing The Committee to Re-elect
Bill Honig, a committee controlled by Bill Honig, California
Superintendent of Public Instruction.  I advised you that Mr. Honig
has been asked to attend events sponsored by the Friends of Langley
Porter Psychiatric Institute, located at the University of
California San Francisco Medical Center and by the Exploratorium,
also located in San Francisco.  Both of these entities are
charitable, non-profit organizations, exempt from tax under Section
501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.  We also discussed specific
dates.  It is anticipated that Mr. Honig will receive some
recognition for his position at both events, which are to be held
very soon.  I advised you that both entities provide programs that
are well within the broad scope of concern of the state educational
system.

I also described to you that Mr. Honig would like to make
gifts to his principal staff assistants in his state office during
the holiday season.

I ask for confirmation as to whether Mr. Honig may use funds
from his re-election committee for the above purposes.

You advised me that, under the circumstances I described to
you, it is permissible for Mr. Honig to use his campaign funds to
attend the Friends of Langley Porter Psychiatric Institute and
Exploratorium events. You indicated that since these activities
are both charitable in nature and reasonably related to a
political, governmental, or legislative purpose, the expenditures
would be permitted under the personal use statutes (Government Code
Section 85800 et. seq) within the Political Reform Act as amended.

You further advised me that gifts from campaign funds made to
employees or campaign committee workers, or to an employee of the
elected officer's agency, are considered under Government Code
§85802(f)(2) to be directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose if the gifts total less than $100 in a
calendar year to each such employee. I asked for clarification as
to whether a gift of $100 or more would be permitted. You advised
me in a telephone call on October 29, 1990, that Government Code
§85802(f)(1) prohibits any such compensation to public employees,
with the exception of less than $100 gifts described above

Thank you very much for your advice. I have advised my client
accordingly and, due to the timing of the impending events, Mr.
Honig may proceed to pay for such events in reliance on your
advice.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Peter A. Bagatelos

PAB/cc
cc: Honorable Bill Honig

honig:morodomi.ltr

sections.question_synthetic: A lawyer seeks confirmation of FPPC oral advice regarding whether campaign funds can be used for a candidate to attend charitable events and to give holiday gifts to state employees.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is incoming correspondence from a lawyer confirming prior telephone advice from FPPC staff (Mark Morodomi), not an official FPPC advice letter response. The document contains the lawyer's recitation of facts and the advice given orally, but lacks formal FPPC letterhead or official response structure. Document is seeking written confirmation of oral guidance rather than presenting a new question for FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: A lawyer seeks confirmation of FPPC oral advice regarding whether campaign funds can be used for a candidate to attend charitable events and to give holiday gifts to state employees.

embedding.summary: Attorney Peter Bagatelos writes to confirm oral legal advice provided by FPPC Legal Division regarding the permissibility of using re-election committee funds for Bill Honig (California Superintendent of Public Instruction) to attend charitable events and give gifts to staff.
